descriptions.name: desc.CREATION
descriptions.en_content: All beings, places and things that exist apart from The GODHEAD as a result of Divine Creation.

descriptions.name: desc.The GODHEAD
descriptions.en_content: The Being of God as existing in Three Persons.

descriptions.name: desc.NULL_TOPIC
descriptions.en_content: This is the NULL TOPIC: the root of the Book of Wisdom's graph database.

descriptions.name: desc.DIVINE SOVEREIGNTY
descriptions.en_content: The theological concept that God has absolute control over all things, meaning everything that happens in the universe occurs according to His plan and will, signifying His supreme authority and power over creation; essentially, God is the ultimate ruler with complete dominion over all aspects of existence.

descriptions.name: desc.FAITHFULNESS
descriptions.en_content: Adherence to something to which one is bound by a pledge or duty.

descriptions.name: desc.HOLINESS
descriptions.en_content: The quality or state of being holy; a Being (God) Who is exalted and worthy of complete devotion as One perfect in goodness and righteousness; a human devoted entirely to the Deity or the Work of the Deity; having a Divine quality; that object which is venerated as sacred.

descriptions.name: desc.LOVE
descriptions.en_content: A strong affection for another arising out of kinship or personal ties; attraction based on affection and tenderness; affection based on admiration, benevolence, or common interests; an assurance of affection; warm attachment, enthusiasm, or devotion; the object of attachment, devotion, or admiration.

descriptions.name: desc.SPIRITUALITY
descriptions.en_content: Sensitivity or attachment to the values of the Holy Spirit of God.

descriptions.name: desc.THE GOSPEL
descriptions.en_content: The Life, Death, Burial, Resurrection, Ascension and Return of Jesus Christ.

descriptions.name: desc.TRUTH
descriptions.en_content: A transcendent fundamental or spiritual reality.

descriptions.name: desc.HUMANITY
descriptions.en_content: Mankind, male and female, created in the Image and Likeness of The GODHEAD.

descriptions.name: desc.WEALTH
descriptions.en_content: The abundance of valuable material, possessions or resources.

descriptions.name: desc.ATTITUDE
descriptions.en_content: A feeling, emotion or mental position to a fact or state.

descriptions.name: desc.WISDOM
descriptions.en_content: A wise attitude, belief, or course of action.

descriptions.name: desc.UNDERSTANDING
descriptions.en_content: A mental grasp of truth or an agreement between individuals or groups.

descriptions.name: desc.HUMILITY
descriptions.en_content: Not having or showing any feelings of superiority, self-assertiveness, or showiness.

descriptions.name: desc.WORSHIP
descriptions.en_content: To honor or show reverence for as The Divine Being; to regard with great or extravagant respect, honor, or devotion; to perform or take part in worship or an act of worship (i.e., falling on one's knees or face in humility and obeisance).

descriptions.name: desc.MORALITY
descriptions.en_content: A doctrine or system of moral conduct.

descriptions.name: desc.EVIL
descriptions.en_content: The fact of suffering, misfortune, and wrongdoing; a cosmic wicked force; something or someone that brings sorrow, distress, or calamity; one that steals, kills and destroys; that angelic being which is the fountain of pride (Lucifer, Satan, the Devil)

descriptions.name: desc.RELIGION
descriptions.en_content: A personal or institutionalized system of religious attitudes, beliefs, and practices.

descriptions.name: desc.PSYCHOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: The study of mind and behavior in relation to a particular field of knowledge or activity.

descriptions.name: desc.ANTHROPOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: The study of human beings and their ancestors through time and space and in relation to physical character, environmental and social relations, and culture.

descriptions.name: desc.COSMOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: The study of the social belief systems humans have held across different times and places. Scientific cosmology involves studying the universe through astronomy and physics.

descriptions.name: description.MUSIC
descriptions.en_content: The art and science of combining vocal or instrumental sounds to produce beauty of form, harmony, and expression of emotion.

descriptions.name: description.MUSICOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: The scholarly study of music, encompassing the historical, cultural, theoretical, and scientific aspects of music and its role in human society.

descriptions.name: desc.FINANCE
descriptions.en_content: Money or other liquid resources of a government, business, group, or individual; the system that includes the circulation of money, the granting of credit, the making of investments, and the provision of banking facilities; the science or study of the management of funds; the obtaining of funds or capital.

descriptions.name: desc.GEOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: Study of the solid matter of a celestial body.

descriptions.name: desc.GRACE
descriptions.en_content: Unmerited Divine Favor given to humans for their sanctification and regeneration; the same Favor God the Father bestows on His Only-Begotten Son Jesus Christ.

descriptions.name: desc.HISTORY
descriptions.en_content: An account of important events in the order in which they happened

descriptions.name: desc.HUMOR
descriptions.en_content: The mental faculty of discovering, expressing, or appreciating the ludicrous or absurdly incongruous; the ability to be funny or to be amused by things that are funny; something that is or is designed to be comical or amusing.

descriptions.name: desc.LAW
descriptions.en_content: A binding custom or practice of a community formally recognized as binding or enforced by a controlling authority.

descriptions.name: desc.LINGUISTICS
descriptions.en_content: The study of human speech including the units, nature, structure, and modification of language.

descriptions.name: desc.MERCY
descriptions.en_content: Compassion or forbearance shown to an offender or to one subject to another’s power.

descriptions.name: desc.PHILOSOPHY
descriptions.en_content: A foundational discipline within the Humanities. It explores fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, values, reason, mind, and language. As such, it provides a framework for understanding other humanities fields like literature, history, art, and religion. While philosophy can be considered a standalone discipline, it also intersects and informs many other areas of study within the humanities.

descriptions.name: desc.POLITICAL-SCIENCE
descriptions.en_content: Exalting one nation above all others and placing primary emphasis on promotion of its culture and interests as opposed to those of other nations.

descriptions.name: desc.SOCIOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: The science of society, social institutions, and social relationships.

descriptions.name: desc.SPIRITS
descriptions.en_content: The animating or vital Principle held to give life to physical organisms; supernatural being or essence.

descriptions.name: desc.THE-BIBLE
descriptions.en_content: The Sacred Scriptures of Christians comprising the Old Testament and the New Testament.

descriptions.name: desc.FIN-GOV
descriptions.en_content: Government finances include revenues, expenditures (spending), debt, and assets (cash and security holdings).

descriptions.name: desc.FREEDOM
descriptions.en_content: The absence of necessity, coercion, or constraint in choice or action with the framework of morality and law.

descriptions.name: desc.JUSTICE
descriptions.en_content: The practice or instance of giving to others what is their due.

descriptions.name: desc.GUILT
descriptions.en_content: A feeling of responsibility for wrongdoing; a courtroom conviction.

descriptions.name: desc.FAITH
descriptions.en_content: Confidence in the Words of God that inspires intent, speech and action.

descriptions.name: desc.NATURAL SCIENCES
descriptions.en_content: Any of the sciences (such as physics, chemistry, or biology) that deal with matter, energy, and their interrelations and transformations or with objectively measurable phenomena.

descriptions.name: desc.BIOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: A branch of knowledge that deals with living organisms and vital processes.

descriptions.name: desc.COMMUNICATION THEORY
descriptions.en_content: A systematic, interdisciplinary study of how information is created, encoded, transmitted, received, and interpreted across human, technological, and symbolic systems.

descriptions.name: desc.ASTROPHYSICS
descriptions.en_content: A branch of astronomy dealing especially with the behavior, physical properties, and dynamic processes of celestial objects and phenomena.

descriptions.name: desc.PHYSICS
descriptions.en_content: The physical properties and composition of matter.

descriptions.name: desc.ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE
descriptions.en_content: The branch of the Natural Sciences that studies how the solid Earth, its water, its air, and its living organisms interact, and how these interconnected systems change over time.

descriptions.name: desc.HEALTH SCIENCES
descriptions.en_content: The interdisciplinary field that studies the human body, health, disease, and healthcare systems in order to prevent illness, improve well‑being, and deliver effective clinical and public‑health interventions.

descriptions.name: desc.BOTANY
descriptions.en_content: The scientific study of plants—their structure, function, reproduction, evolution, classification, and interactions with the environment.

descriptions.name: desc.SOTERIOLOGY
descriptions.en_content: The branch of theology that studies the nature, purpose, and process of salvation—how it is achieved, how it is applied to humanity, and how it ultimately transforms both people and creation.

descriptions.name: desc.HEALTH
descriptions.en_content: The condition of being sound in body, mind, or spirit; freedom from physical disease or pain; the general condition of the body.

descriptions.name: desc.SOCIAL SCIENCES
descriptions.en_content: The group of academic disciplines that systematically study human behavior, social relationships, institutions, and societies using empirical, analytical, and interpretive methods to explain how people interact and how social systems function.
